name: Template-Based File Generation

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Template-Based File Generation is complete and correct

scope:
Template-based file generation per spec .wolfcastle/docs/specs/2026-03-22T09-00Z-template-file-generation.md

Move all generated file content (scaffold, ADRs, specs, task markdown) from Go string literals into template files under internal/project/templates/. Extend the existing PromptRepository/tierfs infrastructure to support a templates/ prefix alongside prompts/ -- do NOT create a parallel system. Add a shared RenderToFile convenience method. Replace all strings.Builder / fmt.Sprintf / os.WriteFile chains at each call site with RenderToFile. Verify with snapshot tests: output must be byte-for-byte identical before and after. After implementation, document the template override mechanism in docs/humans/configuration.md and update relevant CLI docs and CONTRIBUTING.md.
success_criteria: All file-generation call sites (scaffold_service.go, cmd/adr_create.go, cmd/spec.go, project_create.go, cmd/task/add.go) use RenderToFile instead of inline string construction
Snapshot tests verify byte-for-byte identical output before and after migration
Template override mechanism works through the existing three-tier system (base < custom < local)
No parallel template loading system created; PromptRepository extended, not duplicated
Documentation covers template customization in configuration.md, CLI docs, and CONTRIBUTING.md

breadcrumbs.text: Created 3 children: template-repository-infrastructure (leaf, 4 tasks), call-site-migration (orchestrator with 2 leaf children: scaffold-templates 3 tasks, artifact-templates 4 tasks), template-documentation (leaf, 2 tasks). Ordering: infrastructure first (foundation for all migration work), then call-site migration (depends on ResolveTemplate and RenderToFile), then documentation (describes what was built). Found relevant specs: tierfs-resolver-contract, scaffoldservice-contract, and the primary template-file-generation spec. Referenced all three in tasks.
